description:
Goal: make Compiler2 semantic convergence honest by modeling each activation input as the join of caller contributions in the work graph. Signal: the quicksort trace-summary shows no double activation, no duplicate settled publications, no dead transient activations in the final closure path, no pre-drive runtime parsing, and the docs describe activation input as a value fact rather than side-store mutation. Strategy: derive stable recursion and dispatch-key facts first, make fact slots value-aware, move activation input into multi-owner fact aggregation, collapse semantic closure into a read-only predicate, then add engine safety/signal, lazy runtime bootstrap, and docs before backend work resumes.

This epic is driven by ../five-problems.html. The central move is that Activation(root, fn, key).input is not an imperative update in ActivationMap; it is the refine_widen join of every caller-owned contribution. Once that is true, the closure walk, double activation, revision-counter fingerprint, and per-activate recursion peek stop being necessary machinery.
title: Compiler2 fixpoint: model activation inputs as fact joins
